title: Russian Constitutional Court death penalty moratorium cases
source_name: Russian Constitutional Court death penalty moratorium cases
citation: Constitutional Court of the Russian Federation, death penalty jurisprudence
text:
Title: Russian Constitutional Court death penalty moratorium cases
Citation: Constitutional Court of the Russian Federation, death penalty jurisprudence
Jurisdiction: russia
Source URL: http://ksrf.ru/

The Constitutional Court's death penalty jurisprudence tied capital punishment to jury trial guarantees and later maintained a moratorium. The line of authority is relevant to constitutional rights, criminal punishment, human dignity, and Russia's constitutional treatment of capital punishment.

title: Anchugov and Gladkov v Russia
text:
Title: Anchugov and Gladkov v Russia
Citation: ECtHR, Applications nos. 11157/04 and 15162/05
Jurisdiction: russia
Source URL: https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/

The European Court of Human Rights held that Russia's blanket prisoner voting ban violated Article 3 of Protocol No. 1. The case is important for Russian constitutional interaction with international human rights judgments and later Constitutional Court responses.

title: Yukos v Russia
citation: ECtHR, OAO Neftyanaya Kompaniya Yukos v Russia
text:
Title: Yukos v Russia
Citation: ECtHR, OAO Neftyanaya Kompaniya Yukos v Russia
Jurisdiction: russia
Source URL: https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/

The ECtHR examined tax enforcement and fair trial complaints arising from proceedings against Yukos. The case is important for property rights, due process, enforcement measures, and investment disputes connected to Russian state action.

title: Konstantin Markin v Russia
text:
Title: Konstantin Markin v Russia
Citation: ECtHR Grand Chamber, Application no. 30078/06
Jurisdiction: russia
Source URL: https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/

The Grand Chamber found discrimination in denial of parental leave to a male serviceman. The case became significant in Russian constitutional dialogue over ECtHR judgments, equality, military service, and family rights.
